MACRO REGIME — 2026-07-01 (all facts from feed scripts run this session)

1. Fed chair Kevin Warsh dodged rate-path questions in his first public remarks of Q3; U.S. stocks edged lower (S&P 500 −0.2%) with investors positioned ahead of Thursday's jobs report [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/u-s-stocks-edge-lower-as-warsh-dodges-questions-on-rates-026f6f58 (2026-07-01)].
2. Warsh vowed "no changes" to Fed independence and said price stability remains the focus regardless of White House pressure — hawkish-opacity regime persists [source: https://www.ft.com/content/e6b33b7c-c730-4818-b132-fc5f52845065 (2026-07-01)].
3. Rotation continues: bank stocks rallied while chips pulled back in a mixed session [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/bank-stocks-rally-chips-pull-back-in-mixed-day-of-trading-34a1548a (2026-07-01)]; Michael Burry disclosed new AI shorts (Tesla, Caterpillar, Applied Materials, a chip ETF), calling it "the beginning of the end" [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/michael-burry-cites-beginning-of-the-end-with-new-ai-short-bets-50793c12 (2026-07-01)].
4. The debasement-trade unwind is pausing, not reversing: gold and silver rose for a second consecutive session to start Q3 after June's ~12% gold drawdown [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/commodities-futures/gold-holds-steady-in-bearish-consolidation-zone-97e170d4 (2026-07-01)]; Treasury yields rose ahead of payrolls [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/investing/jgbs-fall-tracking-declines-in-u-s-treasurys-ff5b8ed9 (2026-07-01)].
5. Risk appetite remains high in primary markets — record $2.8tn H1 dealmaking driven by AI repositioning [source: https://www.ft.com/content/000d10d6-cdfb-4110-9c6b-a5cd133b7a0f (2026-07-01)], record H1 IPO haul with Bending Spoons +40% on debut [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/aol-owner-to-go-public-at-over-18-billion-valuation-d00f302c (2026-07-01)]; oil extended declines on supply optimism as Hormuz traffic normalized [source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/commodities-futures/oil-futures-rise-on-possible-technical-recovery-c1553e8b (2026-07-01)].

READ-THROUGH: Late-cycle AI tape (record dealmaking + famous shorts appearing), hawkish-opaque Fed into a binary jobs print, banks>chips rotation, hard assets stabilizing after the June unwind. Favors: trim extended AI/chip winners into strength, keep debasement basket capped but stop panic-selling it, deploy cash gradually (staged), prefer equal-weight/value/international over Mag7-heavy cap-weight.
